topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic cup felt slick in my palm, the results glowing a stark, undeniable green. My breath hitched, catching somewhere in my throat like broken glass. I stared at the screen, the letters blurring into meaningless shapes, yet the implications slammed into me with physical force. This wasn't a simple confirmation; it was a verdict, a rewriting of everything I thought I knew about my origins. My hands began to shake so violently I had to grip the edge of the desk to steady myself. A cold sweat prickled my hairline, and I could feel the blood draining from my face. Who were they? What kind of secret had been hidden beneath the surface of my life? I wanted to scream, but no sound emerged; my mouth was dry, locked shut by a rising tide of sheer disbelief and dread.

[story 2]

I reread the report for the tenth time, my eyes refusing to settle on the data. Each line of genetic information felt like a tiny, sharp accusation. My stomach twisted into a knot, tight and agonizing. I paced the small room, the floorboards protesting beneath my bare feet with every frantic step. I kept glancing toward the closed door, half expecting someone to burst in, to snatch the device away. My knuckles were white where I clenched my fists, and I could feel a strange pressure building behind my eyes, a dizzying imbalance. Every rational thought fled, replaced by a churning, nauseous certainty that the foundation of my existence had just crumbled into dust.

[story 3]

The silence in the apartment was deafening, amplifying the frantic drumming of my own pulse in my ears. I sat on the edge of the sofa, the printed document spread across my lap, the ink seeming to mock me with its cold, factual pronouncements. My vision tunneled slightly at the edges. I tried to breathe deeply, inhaling the stale air, but the oxygen felt thin, insufficient. A tremor ran through my entire body, starting in my toes and surging upward, making my limbs feel unstable. I squeezed my eyes shut, a desperate attempt to block out the impossible truth laid bare before me, the realization settling in my chest like a crushing weight.
